title: Fixing Middleware Order for a Simple Express API

statement.markdown: Write a simple Express API that handles GET requests to the root path. The API should return a JSON response with the message 'Hello, World!' and the current timestamp. However, the current implementation has a logical bug in the middleware order.
statement.inputFormat: A single GET request to the root path (/)
statement.outputFormat: A JSON response with the message 'Hello, World!' and the current timestamp
statement.notes: The middleware order is critical in this implementation.

constraints.description: The timestamp should be within this range

examples.explanation: The expected response includes the current timestamp

hints.text: Check the order of the middleware

editorial.title: Fixing Middleware Order for a Simple Express API
editorial.intuition: The middleware order affects the order in which the functions are executed. In this case, the timestamp middleware should be executed before the response middleware.
editorial.bruteForce: Try different middleware orders to see which one works
editorial.optimalSolution: The correct middleware order is to execute the timestamp middleware before the response middleware
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The timestamp middleware should be executed before the response middleware to ensure the correct timestamp is returned
editorial.dryRun: Try the dry run example to see the expected output
commonMistakes: Executing the response middleware before the timestamp middleware
